text:
The Succession Contest arc (, Keishō-sen-hen), also known as the Succession to the Throne arc (, Ōi Keishō-hen), is the seventh and current story arc of the series.

The Zodiacs are caught off guard when the Kakin Empire's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou declares his intent to explore the Dark Continent on a voyage led by Beyond Netero, son of the late Isaac Netero on the internet. Ging and Pariston request to leave the Zodiacs and Cheadle accepts, inviting Leorio and Kurapika to the Zodiacs as their replacement. The V5 contract the Hunter Association to hunt Beyond, but after Kakin accepts their invitation to reorganize into the V6, have the association be Beyond's chaperone to the Dark Continent instead. 

Unbeknownst to the public, the Black Whale 1 is where King Nasubi plans to decide who succeeds the throne, through a deathmatch between the fourteen princes of the Kakin Empire. Several of Kakin's Mafia families are on board too, hoping to help their sponsors and further their goals. Kurapika boards, hoping to recover the last of his clan’s Scarlet Eyes from the 4 th Prince of the Kakin Empire, Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou. The Phantom Troupe, having originally boarded the Black Whale 1 to steal its riches, unexpectedly find themselves chasing after Hisoka, who had killed two of their members after his deathmatch with Chrollo.

Summary

The Special Assignment

The Zodiacs (less Ging and Pariston) meet at Cheadle's request. Before the meeting begins, Beans barges into the room and shows them a video of the Kakin Empire's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou declaring his country's intent to travel to the Dark Continent. The Zodiacs are surprised, more so they see King Nasubi introduce the leader of his expedition: Beyond Netero, the son of the late Isaac Netero. Beyond's heartfelt proclamations quash any doubts the Zodiacs have of the legitimacy of Beyond's claimed lineage. Beans shares a second video, one left by Netero, to be played only if someone claiming to be his child came forth, and only to be played with all members of the Zodiac present. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 340 

Elsewhere, Beyond and his team gather to observe the public's reaction to their announcement. When a team member brings up the V5's attempts to stop him, Beyond tells him to ignore them and instead speaks of his desire to explore new land to his team, which includes the former Zodiac, Pariston. Cheadle takes this chance to segue into her reasons for calling the meeting. She tells the Zodiacs of Ging and Pariston's departures from the Zodiacs, and of the special assignment given directly from the V5: Hunt down Beyond. 

The International Permit Agency (IPA) director leads a newly appointed deputy secretary to the basement of one of their agency's buildings and along the way discusses the difficulty they have in stopping Kakin's journey. At the basement, the deputy secretary looks in horror at tanks filled with biological atrocities, which the director explains are the remnants of humanity's past attempts to colonize the New World. The director passes a copy of a book to the deputy secretary, explains that it contains travels that were published hundreds of years ago, and instructs him to read it and make a report identifying the risks of traveling there. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 341 

The Zodiacs watch a video of Isaac Netero discussing his past experiences with the Dark Continent, which ends with Netero "requesting" the Zodiacs overcome the risks of the Dark Continent and return with hope, successfully exploring the Dark Continent before Beyond. While the Zodiacs debate how to do so, they are contacted by Beyond, who turns himself in. Despite being restrained, Beyond leads the interview between him and Cheadle. He boldly predicts that the Zodiacs will release him and accompany him to the Dark Continent. Beyond explains the V5's official policy and what the "real" world is to the Zodiacs. At a summit with the heads of the V5, the IPA's deputy secretary explains that the official ways to stop Kakin are limited to military, and proposes an alternative: Invite Kakin to join the V5, reorganize as the V6, and unofficially support Kakin's journey. Find a fake "Dark Continent" and colonize it to maintain public appearances, and let Kakin tempt fate as they desire. The heads of the V5 accept this proposal and sign their agreement. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 342 

Beyond explains to Cheadle that his actions were a way to force the V5 to act, the act of leading the journey itself a way for Beyond to explore the Dark Continent in exchange for helping King Nasubi leave his mark on history. The Zodiacs begin making preparations to simultaneously handle both the Dark Continent and Beyond's attempts to escape their custody once there. Elsewhere, the IPA director reassures the deputy secretary that his choice to have the V5 accept a peaceful resolution over a safe conclusion during the briefing was right. At the base of Beyond's Dark Continent Expedition Team, programs over the television introduce the Black Whale, the ship for the voyage, to the public and discuss the threats found on the Dark Continent. Ging steps into the base, and asks to join the expedition team. 

Voyage Preparations

The 289 th Hunter Exam and the Dark Continent Expedition Team

Cheadle contacts Leorio and invites him to be a Zodiac owing to his popularity during the election. He agrees, and asks if there is a free spot for Kurapika. Elsewhere, Ging's argument with Pariston heats up. Team members step in to stop them, but Ging quickly intimidates them into accepting him as their team's deputy leader. Mizaistom heads to a base of the Nostrade Family and restrains some thugs using his Cross Game to strongarm Kurapika into a conversation. Kurapika agrees to join the Zodiacs and accompany them to the Dark Continent in exchange for the identity of the owner of a large amount of Scarlet Eyes, the 4 th Prince of the Kakin Empire, Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 343 

At Beyond's team's base, Ging tries various ways to ease new tensions, including offering to double their payment and demonstrating his knowledge of the Dark Continent. Some members leave, and some begrudgingly accept him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 344 Ging steps out to have a short call with Gon, who can no longer use his Nen. He tells Gon to be grateful and accept that he has returned to normal. Gon tells Mito why he chose not to travel with Ging, and is forced to catch up in his education. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 345 

Leorio reunites with Kurapika briefly before the Zodiacs' meeting. Cheadle shares that Kakin is now a member of the V6, gives a quick overview of the reason past expeditions failed, and states their objectives for their journey. When Kurapika asks if Beyond's allies within the Hunter Association had been identified, Mizaistom leads him out and tells him that he and Cheadle had not yet concluded that none of the Zodiacs were secretly allied with Beyond. The meeting resumes and the Zodiacs introduce their individual roles on the voyage to each other. Cheadle ends the meeting by announcing the beginning of the 289 th Hunter Exam, this year only allowing people recruited for the journey to pass. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 346 

A month later, members of Beyond's team return to their base after failing the 289 th Hunter Exam. In feigned annoyance they threaten Ging and Pariston and ask them to decide who between them leaves. They chase Ging and Pariston down a tunnel, and Ging gets rid of them. After defeating them, he tells Pariston that he would have showed him his ability if he asked, that there was no need to put on a show. With this, Ging is accepted as the team's deputy leader. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 347 

Kurapika oversees the final phase of the 289 th Hunter Exam, using his Dowsing Chain to identify Beyond's men. Mizaistom praises his ability, but Kurapika asserts that someone with a similar ability could counter his. Mizaistom expresses that there probably is no one within the Zodiacs like that. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 348 

Tserriednich receives a call from his older brother, 1 st Prince Benjamin Hui Guo Rou, who shares that their father had declared a succession war on the voyage, with the winner being the next king of the Kakin Empire. Overjoyed by the news, Tserriednich declares that victory would be his while taunting his older brother. He gets a call from his subordinates, telling him they had passed the Hunter Exam. 

After the 289 th Hunter Exam, Kurapika, and Mizaistom return to the possibility of a mole among the Zodiacs. Under the guise of promoting cooperation on the journey, Mizaistom summons the senior Zodiacs and convinces them to share their abilities. Kurapika watches a recording and uses his Dowsing Chain to identify Saiyu as a spy for Beyond. 

The Princes and the Seed Urn Ritual

King Nasubi's personal butler Nugui explains to Tserriednich that the Succession War would begin once the voyage began, but would be canceled if any of the participants (limited to the children of the King's legal wives) died before. Tserriednich then participates in a ritual, receiving, unbeknownst to him, an "egg" which will hatch into a personal Guardian Spirit Beast, influenced by his personality. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 

The Zodiacs and Beyond

Kurapika watches a video where Mizaistom offers Beyond the chance to participate in the eve festival and departure ceremony in exchange for the identity of his spy in the Zodiacs. Beyond denies any knowledge of a spy and any interest in the ceremony, which Kurapika verifies with his Dowsing Chain. Since Beyond is unaware of Saiyu, Kurapika and Mizaistom conclude that Pariston is the mastermind and had conspired with Saiyu, and decide that the safest course of action would be to restrain Saiyu shortly before their arrival on the Dark Continent. 

Kurapika infiltrates the Princes' bodyguards

As they leave the recording room, Kurapika receives a call from Linssen, and Cheadle speaks with Mizaistom. Coincidentally, the two conversations have the same information. Kurapika learns that six of Kakin's Princes are using the Kakin government website to recruit bodyguards for the voyage, wasting their efforts to weed out spies during the latest Hunter Exam since failed examinees would still be able to travel to the Dark Continent and assist Beyond by applying to be bodyguards for the Princes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 

Kurapika decides to maximize his chances of reaching Tserriednich by recruiting Basho, Biscuit, Hanzo, Izunavi, and Melody to each apply as a bodyguard to one of the six listings. Kurapika lets the others know that he desires close proximity to Tserriednich or information that would help him do so, and leaves the means to the discretion of the others. Using what little information he can interpret from the six anonymous listings and the Princes' relations, Kurapika applies to what he believes is 9 th Prince Halkenburg's listing. Kurapika reminds the others to share any information learned with him immediately and to use Q in case of requests for information privacy, and the group split up. 

Later that day, Kurapika meets his future employer at one of the Kakin Empire's hotels, and is disappointed to learn it is 8 th Queen Oito, mother of 14 th Prince Woble, a baby. Oito is relieved to see his disappointment, having hoped to find someone who both could interpret the posting details of her listing as Halkenburg and seek to work for "Halkenburg". She reveals the reason for the posting restrictions on the listings, to both protect Princes from being easily identified and exposed to assassins, and prevent the popular Princes from dominating the applicant crowd. Halkenburg, to whom both scenarios would apply, chose not to hire bodyguards through the Kakin government website. Oito senses Kurapika's confusion at why assassins would seek to be bodyguards, and reveals the true nature of the succession war — a deathmatch with only one survivor. The superior Princes (Princes 1-4) would have their own private forces ready for the match. Only the younger or less aware Princes would need to resort to hiring people at the last minute to level the playing field. Oito explains what it means for one to be a Prince/Queen in the eyes of King Nasubi, either a religious belief in oneself/one's child in becoming King, or a tragic fate should anyone choose to believe otherwise. She explains the younger Princes' reliance on Halkenburg and the effect of participating in the succession war on his reputation to ensure their safety in the event Halkenburg wins. 

Having explained the situation, Oito offers Kurapika a choice between accepting her request for tenfold compensation, or rejecting her request and keeping the details a secret in exchange for the original compensation. Kurapika agrees to accept her request on condition that she helps him get close to Tserriednich, and the Queen and Kurapika come to an agreement. She reminds Kurapika about the implicit social standing between the Queens and Princes that hides behind the equal outward appearances. She reminisces about her life and lets Kurapika hold Woble. The employers of the other hunters Kurapika hired are revealed — Izunavi's employer 6 th Prince Tyson, Biscuit's 13 th Prince Marayam, Basho's 7 th Prince Luzurus, Melody's 10 th Prince Kacho, and Hanzo's 12 th Prince Momoze. 

Heavens Arena: Battle to the Death

At Heavens Arena, Hisoka and Chrollo (now a Floor Master) agree to a deathmatch. A referee begins the fight, and Chrollo stabs him with an antenna, using Shalnark's Black Voice to attack Hisoka. Hisoka realizes that Chrollo was using the time spent fleeing to prepare more abilities. Chrollo uses the referee to try and pin Hisoka down to stab him with Black Voice 's second antenna and control him. Hisoka is forced on the defensive by the 2v1 but sees an opening and cuts down the referee. The referee suddenly explodes and Hisoka dodges by using his Bungee Gum to pull himself towards the ceiling. Chrollo reveals two abilities: The Sun and Moon, which allows him to use his left and right hands to imprint opposing seals on targets that explode on contact with each other, and Double Face, a special bookmark allowing him to bookmark a page in Skill Hunter's book to maintain a page's ability without having to keep it open, letting him either use an ability with both hands free, or use two abilities simultaneously by having one page bookmarked and the other open. Chrollo tells Hisoka he would show him three more abilities, adding that he should be proud to be the person needing the most abilities ever to kill. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 351 

Chrollo introduces Order Stamp, explaining its ability to control copies of corpses. Chrollo flips to another page and bookmarks it with Double Face, then uses Kortop i's Gallery Fake to make a copy of the referee's corpse, then demonstrates Order Stamp 's functionality by stamping the copy, turning it into a puppet, and ordering it to "break Hisoka". Hisoka beheads the puppet, confirming that beheading is sufficient to deactivate Order Stamp. Chrollo tells Hisoka that when the copy vanishes he may conclude that Gallery Fake has been deactivated. The copy vanishes as Chrollo deactivates Gallery Fake to show and explain Convert Hands, the final ability prepared for their deathmatch, an ability which allows him to give someone his appearance by touching them with his right hand and take theirs with his left hand. Before resuming the fight, Chrollo reveals that one of his abilities belongs to someone who is already dead. Hisoka understands the implication and Chrollo confirms that the marks from The Sun and Moon are a form of post-mortem Nen: they will never disappear unless they explode. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 352 

Chrollo predicts and declares his victory, offering Hisoka a chance to stop, but Hisoka continues without hesitation. Chrollo jumps into the audience and manipulates two spectators with Black Voice, having them engage Hisoka to buy him time to disguise himself with Convert Hands. The audience starts to panic and flee the arena, and the commentator tries to direct them to exit without rushing. Hisoka starts seeing clones from Gallery Fake in the audience, and they start rushing toward him because of Order Stamp. Hisoka uses Bungee Gum to pull himself toward the ceiling, away from the puppets. The puppets stack up in a wave of bodies and force Hisoka to defend himself, leaving an opening Chrollo exploits to land a hit on him. Despite being pummeled, Hisoka enjoys the fight. He has his hands full just decapitating the puppets, and Chrollo uses openings to land guaranteed hits. Hisoka eventually successfully anticipates Chrollo's attack and flings a decapitated head at Chrollo using Bungee Gum. Chrollo dodges it easily and attacks Hisoka, but fails to notice a second head Hisoka sent flying at him and is hit. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 353 

Chrollo manages to recover from Hisoka's sneak attack while keeping Skill Hunter open, letting him keep his puppets. Hisoka continues decapitating the remaining puppets. He spots and attacks Chrollo, and Chrollo chooses to retreat into the audience. Hisoka restrains one puppet to see if Chrollo would choose to make more puppets or use his remaining ones. The stamp vanishes from the puppet Hisoka is restraining, which leads Hisoka to believe Chrollo chose to deactivate Order Stamp in order to use both hands to make more puppets with Gallery Fake before reactivating Order Stamp and controlling his new puppets. Hisoka scans the audience for signs of commotion, a sign that Chrollo had not re-disguised himself with Convert Hands. In a moment of insight, Hisoka jumps to where Chrollo had other copies on standby, and spots Chrollo in the spectator stands. He sees Chrollo concealing his hands in his clothes and is wary of Black Voice being active and the risk of being pinned by its antenna. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 354 

Hisoka manages to catch Chrollo by using Bungee Gum to send audience members flying at him, and sticks Chrollo's leg with Bungee Gum. He launches a series of strong, unavoidable attacks using a puppet's head, but aura begins to vanish from Chrollo's body and Hisoka discovers it was a disguised and manipulated spectator. Hisoka thinks for a minute about how Convert Hands and Black Voice could be used while the puppet's head remained from Gallery Fake, and quickly realizes that the puppets that had been marked by The Sun and Moon remain even if Gallery Fake had been deactivated. 

Hisoka leaps to the center of the arena to regroup and prepare for a large series of exploding puppets to chase him. He guesses that there were fewer than he initially assumed, since he had not factored in The Sun and Moon. Chrollo sends his puppets to rampage through the audience and cause chaos, which he uses as a cover to steal the commentator's headset and instantaneously command all puppets to break Hisoka. Hundreds of audience members rush toward Hisoka, which he turns into a human hammer. His weapon does not last, and Hisoka returns to using a severed head as his weapon. Chrollo controls a puppet with Black Voice, and waits for Hisoka to hold the head in his hand, before ordering the puppet to touch its mark to the mark on the body of Hisoka's head, blowing his hand off. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 355 

Hisoka takes a moment to understand how the situation unfolded before being forced to act defensively. Hisoka takes a new head to prevent a repeat and runs along to the walls of the second floor of spectator stands using Bungee Gum, sporadically knocking back the wave of human puppets. Chrollo orders puppets to head to the second floor and detonate in front of Hisoka, destroying his leg and sending him flying towards the arena floor. Hisoka tries to use Bungee Gum on his remaining leg to escape to the ceiling but Chrollo throws spectators at him to interrupt him and drop him into the crowd of puppets. Hisoka lands and attempts one final action before being surrounded by a massive explosion. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 356 

A news reporter at the scene of Heavens Arena confirms the end of the deathmatch, which resulted in several audience casualties. Shalnark, Kortopi, and Machi stand over Hisoka's corpse and verify his death. Hisoka's corpse remained mostly intact, because the large crowd of puppets had a cushioning effect that protected him from the bulk of the explosion. Kortopi and Shalnark leave but Machi remains to treat his wounds. Before she can do so, aura begins to surround his corpse, and Hisoka comes back to life; his attempt at resurrecting himself using post-mortem Bungee Gum successful. After reorienting himself, Hisoka restrains Machi using Bungee Gum, telling her he plans to kill all of the Spiders before leaving. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 357 

Shalnark talks on the phone with Chrollo, who plans to have the spiders board the Black Whale to steal its valuables. After he hangs up, Shalnark wonders why Kortopi is taking so long in the toilet. As Shalnark's phone rings, Hisoka exits the public restrooms holding Kortopi's severed head. He throws it at Shalnark, dashes forward, and delivers a powerful strike to his head, killing him before he can react. Hisoka leaves Shalnark's mangled corpse tied to a swing, with Kortopi's severed head placed at the feet of Shalnark's corpse, surrounded by crows. 

The Eve Festival and Departure Ceremony

The day before the voyage arrives, and the eve festival begins. Announcers introduce King Nasubi to the crowd, followed shortly by Beyond and the fourteen Princes. The Princes wave at the crowd and the airship containing King Nasubi lands on the Black Whale, followed shortly by airships containing the other Princes and VIPs and ferries containing general passengers. The eve festival celebrations continue in the banquet hall on Tier 1 of the Black Whale. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

Balsamilco Might, captain of 1 st Prince Benjamin's Royal Guards, briefs his men about the structure of the Black Whale, that the "hunting grounds" are restricted to Tiers 1 and 2, that they are to act as guards for the two months between the day of departure and the completion of the landfall ceremony, and the safety measures taken to ensure the safety of the princes. 

After the banquet, back in their residence of Room 1014, Kurapika discusses ways of escaping the Black Whale with Queen Oito, noting that contact between Queens and Princes was possible via phone. Oito lets Kurapika know that lower wives are unable to call wives above them, and that troops guard the phones. Oito is confused by the King's motives and how he expects the Princes to die, considering the Royal Troops, whom she believes to be a neutral party, are monitoring the passageways and movements of everyone on Tier 1. 

Kurapika decides to identify the strength of the other Princes in relation to Woble's to help form a plan for Woble and Oito's safety. The results of the lax examination conducted by the Dark Continent committee resulted in around 150 temporary hunters with unknown motives roaming around Tier 1, giving Kurapika a bad feeling about the voyage. He gets a call from Mizaistom updating him on the situation on the lower decks, and letting him know that the Zodiacs would likely call off their scheduled meeting due to Kakin's lack of preparation to deal with the general public. Kurapika confirms Oito's emergency evacuation route with her, who reacts strongly to the unexpected possibility of her surviving the trip. Kurapika calls the system controls room to get more information, but senses Nen coming from Woble's direction, surprising him. 

The Succession Contest Begins

Departure of Black Whale 1: The First Day

The Black Whale 1 sets off for the Dark Continent. An announcer announces that the Black Whale 1 will take three weeks to pass through territorial waters, before refueling and venturing into the more volatile uncharted waters for five weeks, before finally reaching the "Dark Continent". In a first-class cabin on Tier 3, Gel is seen briefing the Dark Continent intelligence/research team about the Black Whale 1's real journey. Outside Beyond's cell on Tier 1, Saiyu, Kanzai, and Saccho decide to propose a guarding rotation to Mizaistom, so that one of them may help support the Royal Troops. At the medical care facility at the center of Tier 3, Cheadle and Leorio organize the medicine amidst a hectic situation. At the Kakin Royal Troop's Meeting Room on Tier 4, Mizaistom briefs the Royal Troops. Botobai presides over the central courthouse on Tier 3. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 

The departure ceremony takes place, and the King, Princes, and various VIPs attend. While Oito and Woble attend the ceremony, Kurapika and the other guards find the exsanguinated corpse of fellow guard Woody in a bathroom stall. Kurapika asks the other guards if they can use Nen, but all of them deny knowledge of it. Woody's corpse is taken away, and some guards ask Kurapika why he mentioned Nen, believing their mission simply to protect Oito and Woble, and the succession contest to be an internal conflict of the Kakin Army. Kurapika realizes that he is the only guard that applied knowing about the succession contest. 

The designated time for Woble to leave comes, and she and Oito are escorted out. Benjamin sees them leave and notes that all 14 princes are aboard the ship and participating in the succession war. When 10 th Prince Kacho is invited to leave, she has her sister 11 th Prince Fugetsu follow her, disregarding the measures put in place to ensure Princes leave alone. Kacho has Fugetsu help her put up a front for watching guests, but once out of earshot, covertly orders Fugetsu to help her win in exchange for a pardon. Melody hears Kacho's whispers from outside, having arrived to receive her, but hears her heartbeat and realizes her intolerable behavior is a facade. 

Kurapika and several guards leave to receive Oito and Woble but return to see four more of Woble's bodyguards exsanguinated. Kurapika draws his Dowsing Chain to interrogate the remaining people in the room, getting two guards to admit to prior knowledge of the succession contest and to confess partial allegiance to others. The two are restrained and explain that together with the five murdered bodyguards, the seven of them were to keep tabs on Woble, one for each higher-ranked queen, to protect Woble but ensure she does not become a threat to their Princes. One restrained guard suggests the Seed Urn Ceremony bestowed Woble with Nen abilities. Kurapika wonders if the murders were done by Woble in self-defense, or maliciously by another Prince. Sayird refutes the idea that it was Woble by pointing out the lack of time to develop Nen proficiency, but Bill mentions it would be possible with parasitic Nen, which fits the idea of the Seed Urn ceremony. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

Suddenly, Nen beasts flood the room. Kurapika makes an emergency broadcast using all channels and contacts his fellow Hunters for information. Kurapika learns that Princes are unable to see the Nen beasts, which is contradictory to the idea that Nen users should be able to see Nen beasts. Kurapika calls Bill over and he explains that the parasitic abilities are like curses, using the hosts aura to fuel their ability, felt by their hosts as inexplicable fatigue. The Nen beasts leave the room, but one remains and pesters Sayird, continually asking if he is free. 

Kurapika and Bill begin discussing possible ways for Oito and Woble to escape the succession battle when they hear a servant scream. They rush back and see Sayird holding a bloodied knife over the corpses of three guards. Sayird attacks Bill and Kurapika, but is swiftly restrained by Kurapika's Dowsing Chain. Kurapika uses Steal Chain to extract Sayird's aura, hoping that a parasite would be forced out. A small spider-like Nen beast creeps out of Sayird's ear and attempts escape. Hoping to capture it with Sayird's ability, Kurapika activates his Emperor Time, upgrading Steal Chain into Stealth Dolphin to analyze Sayird's ability. He learns that Sayird's Little Eye is unable to control Nen creatures, and the spider-like Nen creature escapes. Sayird explains how the beast manipulated him. He tells Kurapika more of Little Eye 's rules before being taken away by the Kakin Army. Two of Oito's servants leave, unwilling to continue. Resuming their conversation, Bill says that escaping needs either Pariston or Beyond, both of whom would be difficult to cooperate with. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 361 

In 12 th Prince Momoze's residential area, her mother Queen Sevanti orders many of Momoze's bodyguards and servants to be transferred to her brother 13 th Prince Marayam. The bodyguards are hesitant but defer. Momoze allows her remaining guards to relax as she knits, wondering why she feels unnaturally tired, unable to see the mouse-like Guardian Spirit Beast that had manipulated Sayird sitting quietly at her back. 

At the banquet hall, Halkenburg receives his notice to depart and approaches his father for a private conversation. He announces his wishes to withdraw from the battle to his father, stating he joined merely to save his father's reputation and does not wish to earn the crown through bloodshed. Nasubi tells him to do as he wishes, while observing Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast perched on his shoulders. 

Bill elaborates on Nen parasites, explaining their unpredictability due to the influence of their hosts' personality and emotions. Oito shares her impression that the 1 st through 5 th Princes would never willingly opt-out, prompting Kurapika to declare that the real battle begins after all the Princes return from the ceremony. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 

Back at the departure ceremony, 5 th Prince Tubeppa approaches Tserriednich with a proposal for an alliance against their other siblings. Tubeppa states her belief that the lower-ranked Princes are either unwilling or unable to fight, and the top three Princes have massive character flaws. Tserriednich does not respond to her proposal but agrees with her characterizations, and clinks glasses with her. Tubeppa takes her leave, and Tserriednich wonders if she will be alive by next Sunday. Nasubi observes their Nen beasts, and has a monologue explaining the rules regarding the behavior of Guardian Spirit Beasts: 
Guardian Spirit Beasts may not kill each other. 
Guardian Spirit Beasts may not directly attack others with Guardian Spirit Beasts. 

Guardian Spirit Beasts have these restrictions to protect the future king from fellow Princes with personalities that restrict their respective Guardian Spirit Beasts to ones with only fangs and claws. This lets Princes with talents for indirect action able to see the long path to victory have a chance at winning their succession battle. Nasubi observes 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei, 2 nd Prince Camilla, and Benjamin with their respective Nen beasts. 

Outside Room 1004, provisional hunters Theta and Salkov prepare to receive Tserriednich and discuss how to best explain the Nen beasts mentioned in Kurapika's announcement to him. In their rooms, 7 th Prince Luzurus and 6 th Prince Tyson learn of Nen and their Guardian Spirit Beasts through their bodyguards. Luzurus decides not to bother learning it upon hearing that it could take a year to "see" Nen. Tyson is more concerned about the appearance of her Nen beast than its abilities. 8 th Prince Salé-salé ignores the succession battle entirely, preferring to party in his room. Halkenburg is horrified as to find many of his bodyguards unconscious in his room. Theta and Salkov receive Tserriednich, and Theta is confronted by Tserriednich and his Guardian Spirit Beast. Tserriednich asks Theta if she knows about Nen beasts, and his Guardian Spirit Beast bares its fangs at Theta as it waits for her response. In fear, she explains it pertains to Nen, a special ability she can use. 

Zhang Lei learns of the attack on Woble's team from his guards when they receive him. He remarks his surprise that someone would choose to target the youngest prince first. A bodyguards take the opportunity to bring up the idea of Nen beasts to him, a term his team is unfamiliar with, and suggests that it may be related to the attack on Woble's guards. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 

At the banquet hall, Camilla asks her father to ensure that the only accepted way to drop out of the survival contest is biological death. Nasubi tells her that the sole survivor will be the official successor to the throne, as he told Zhang Lei earlier, and that her interpretation of the phrasing is part of the contest. Benjamin arrives and tells Camilla to not be so focused on others' interpretations of the rules. The two bicker, and Camilla leaves. Benjamin apologizes to their father for her words, and makes an oath to prune the great tree of Kakin's diseased branches and protect it. 

Benjamin takes his leave from the ceremony and asks one of his guards to have Balsamilco bring Tserriednich alive to him for him to kill. In Room 1001, Balsamilco meets Benjamin and advises him to wait due to the possibility of unknown rules governing the behavior of the Guardian Spirit Beasts that Kurapika mentioned over the emergency channel, and the loss of their Nen knowledge advantage because of Kurapika's emergency communication. Balsamilco suggests the announcement was to encourage a stalemate, since the Hunters' goal is to get to the Dark Continent. Ken'i senses Nobunaga's interest in the door, and accepts Nobunaga's offer to kill the culprit in exchange for seeing what's inside. The supervisor, Tsudonke (secretly a fan of the Spiders), introduces himself and shows them around the base. 

At 10 a.m. on the fourth day, an announcement is made on Tier 3 imposing martial law and asking passengers to remain in their cabins due to a stowaway with a deadly weapon on the loose. Botobai leads the sweep through Tier 3 and finds Illumi and Kalluto. Mizaistom confirms with them that all the Spiders are on the Black Whale, and offers them a room in their dorm at the Central Police Station for them to remain on Tier 3 through the martial law. Mizaistom is approached and quietly informed that Fugetsu was found on Tier 3 and rushes back to their headquarters to handle the matter, asking Botobai and Ginta to take charge. On the way, Mizaistom checks that she is safe, asks for a gag order to be imposed, and has the soldiers that recognized her placed into custody. Mizaistom approaches Fugetsu and asks her to help him make sense of the matter. 

In the central police station on Tier 3, Mizaistom confirms that Fugetsu is not in Room 1011 and begins interviewing Fugetsu. Despite Mizaistom's reassurances of confidentiality, Fugetsu refuses to answer Mizaistom, willing to speak to only Kacho. Mizaistom has Melody informed about the situation, warned that Kacho and Fugetsu are likely to plot an escape, and instructed to assist Keeney to shut their plans down. Melody shares the message with Keeney. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 

Mizaistom escorts Fugetsu back to Room 1011. She thinks about how her ability is just like their game as kids, appreciative that Kacho had always let her pick the destination. Cleapatro's investigator informs Fugetsu that she would have an escort for 72 hours, but could still attend the Sunday banquet. Fugetsu apologizes and begins practicing her glass harp. At the same time, Kacho listens to music with earbuds as Melody performs for Seiko and the investigator. Seiko is inspired to arrange for the broadcast to be heard ship-wide. After he leaves, Melody wonders why his heartbeat was contrary to his passionate words, wonders if he is being manipulated, and hopes nothing else happens before the banquet. 

In Room 1008, Rihan notices that a small clone of Salé-salé's Guardian Spirit Beast appears over fellow guard Koroabde's head, answering his final question on the beast's abilities. Rihan activates Predator, summoning a four-legged creature with a giant mouth. It consumes the clone above Koroabde's head, then leaps through the air and consumes Salé-salé's Guardian Spirit Beast whole. Rihan reports the disposal of Salé-salé's Nen beast and requests to sub out for Yushohi. He is notified that Yushohi will be sent over, and to report to Room 1011 after Yushohi's arrival. Rihan hears Yushohi report about Fugetsu's Nen beast's ability, an unfortunate act since having abilities explained instead of deduced reduces Predator's effectiveness. 

Yushohi thinks about how assassins will swarm Fugetsu now that her ability has been discovered. He reflects upon his failed attempt at using How to Get Away with Murder, terminated since he could not notice Fugetsu escaping. Yushohi receives instructions to switch duties with Rihan. At 8:00 p.m. on the fifth day of the voyage, 72 hours until the Sunday banquet, the lockdown order on Tier 3 is lifted. 

Four days after his first visit, Halkenburg once again attempts to see the King. The soldiers have their guns relaxed, expecting yet another letter, but are caught off guard when Halkenburg and his men point guns at them. Halkenburg walks in and sees Nasubi seated alone with his back to Halkenburg, seemingly distracted. Halkenburg demands he suspend the contest. Nasubi tells Halkenburg that the battle for succession is out of his hands, and tells Halkenburg to take action, not be hypocritical. Halkenburg fires a shot at Nasubi. His bullet stops and crumples mid-air. Nasubi takes the bullet, inspects it, and tells Halkenburg that he cannot die until the ritual ends. Nugui enters and informs Halkenburg that he and his guards will not be punished, this scenario being accounted for before the ship's departure as training exercises. Halkenburg aims the gun at himself and shoots, trying to test the rules. His Nen beast stops the bullet mid-air, and Nasubi asks him if he has awakened. Nasubi tells Halkenburg that the people's lives always come first, and that the only way for him to change the country is to become King. Halkenburg abandons his gun, and leaves. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

In Room 1009, Shikaku wakes up to Benjamin contacting him through his earpiece, realizing that Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast must have been the reason for his unnatural sleep. Benjamin tells Shikaku about Halkenburg's failed attempt on Nasubi's life, warns Shikaku about Halkenburg's awakening, and orders him to procure Halkenburg's ability. Shikaku watches Halkenburg ready an aura bow at him and prepares to use Culdecept to defend himself. Halkenburg shoots and penetrates Culdecept, hitting Shikaku. Shikaku sees one of Halkenburg's men collapse, right as he collapses himself. After a moment, Shikaku rises, possessed by Halkenburg's fallen man, and asks Halkenburg for orders. 

During lessons, Kurapika feels this powerful undulation of aura for a second time. In Room 1008, Salé-salé asphyxiates in bed, receiving unsuccessful attempts at resuscitation. Yushohi reports the matter to his superiors. 

At 8 p.m. on the eighth day, the Sunday banquet begins. The emcees announce a concert hosted by the royal family, apologize, and inform the audience that Salé-salé will not perform due to illness. Kacho and Fugetsu are told about the program changes, and note that the order of performances is unchanged. Next to Keeney, Melody nervously awaits the time to execute the plan. In Room 1013, Biscuit, Vergei, and a Hunter guard decide to send the Hunters assigned to Marayam to the "real-world" Room 1013 for it to seem more natural, except for Biscuit who decides to stay with Marayam. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383 

Melody's flute performance is introduced after Keeney finishes his piano performance. Kacho and Fugetsu put earbuds in, pretending to prepare for their act. Melody entrances her listeners into a scenic world of sound. The music plays over the speakers, entrancing even the Princes that are not attending and the guards outside the banquet hall. Keeney brazenly leads Kacho and Fugetsu through open hallways past guards to the lifeboats. Kacho and Fugetsu board a lifeboat and it launches. After the lifeboat disappears from his view, Keeney calmly leaves the lifeboat area and cocks his gun. Melody thinks of her earlier conversation with Keeney, where he volunteers to take the fall for Kacho and Fugetsu's escape attempt, absolving the Princes and the Hunter Association from responsibility. Keeney shoots himself in the head, joining his deceased wife and daughter. 

Fugetsu and Kacho share an emotional moment as the boat passes through the escape tunnel, but their moment is cut short when Kacho has a terrible feeling. She turns back and sees a swarm of shadowy hands coming through the walls and reaching for them. Kacho realizes that leaving the ship means death, and shouts for Fugetsu to open the door right as their lifeboat falls out of the escape tunnel. Fugetsu opens a door back to the ship, and Kacho pushes her in. Fugetsu sees the door shut before Kacho enters, but the door reopens shortly after, and she sees Kacho join her in the tunnel. As they make their way back through Fugetsu's tunnel, Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast and its ability are revealed – if either Kacho or Fugetsu dies, Kacho's formless Guardian Spirit Beast takes her place and protects the other until her death. Kacho's lifeless body drifts out to sea. 

At 10:30 p.m. on the seventh day, Ken'i updates Tajao on the unsuccessful results of their search as they walk through a dim tunnel. They exit the tunnel and Phinks, Feitan, and Nobunaga see Ken'i and Tajao stepping through the mysterious back door. Tajao seats himself at a desk and Ken'i reports the events of the past few days to him, sharing that the hitman responsible for the acts on Tier 3 likely came down with accomplices and were together responsible for about 300 missing workers. Phinks offers to get rid of the hitman in exchange for Tajao's assistance in getting permission to search Tier 4 for Hisoka. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 384 

Tajao tells the Spiders that Morena's absence will lead to an all-out war. Tajao and Ken'i explain the Kakin Mafia's code of conduct and the implications of killing the hitman to the Spiders: 
Members must inform their bosses before making a hit or raid on another family 
Bosses then contact the boss of the other family within 24 hours of the incident to make a deal for compensation. 
Contact can be made after the fact only if the other's boss is away from his turf during the incident, with an unwritten time limit of 24 hours. 
On the Black Whale, only Tiers 1 and 2 are considered away from the Mafia's turf. 
It had been more than two days since the first murder, but Morena may have genuinely been away. 
Morena being on Tier 3 but not contacting Onior or Brocco for this long is a sign of war. 
If the Spiders kill the hitman, the Phantom Troupe will find themselves at war with the Heil-Ly. 
Tajao offers to talk to the Xi-Yu to let the Spiders search for Hisoka on Tier 4. Nobunaga and Phinks decide that if Morena is on the lower tiers, the quickest way to gain access to the other tiers to search for Hisoka is to kill her. They ask Tajao if he can quickly verify Morena's location, and decide to hunt down the hitman in the meantime. Ken'i and Tajao share a look about how dangerous the Spiders are. Onior and Brocco are seen in their rooms on Tier 1, but Morena's room appears empty. 

Rapid Development of the 4 th and 9 th Princes

Over the phone, Tserriednich confirms Morena's absence from the VVIP area. He tells the caller to do as he please, and that he would do something about an unspecified matter when he has time. As his men clean up a mass of human remains, he tells them to get the soldiers in the lower tiers to search Morena's hideout before resuming his Nen training with Theta. While applying the four basic principles, Tserriednich unintentionally conjures a jester-like Nen beast. In fear of what Tserriednich's tremendous potential might produce if given enough time, Theta decides to proactively direct Tserriednich towards learning Zetsu and sets him a target of maintaining Zetsu over his whole body for an hour. Tserriednich's Guardian Spirit Beast glares at Theta, but judges that she is not lying and retreats. Theta watches Tserriednich progress into a state of Zetsu, thinking that she can still be sincere in her desire for him to master Zetsu, and may be able to pull her goals off the next day. 

The next day, 25 minutes before the Sunday banquet, Tserriednich continues practicing Zetsu under Theta's supervision. She manages to distract him and break his Zetsu twice, provoking Tserriednich into obstinately resolving to not slip up again no matter what. Theta watches Tserriednich's Guardian Spirit Beast vanish as he enters Zetsu and prods him with a small bubble of Nen, but Tserriednich does not let up his Zetsu despite Theta's aura bubble popping in his face. Slowly, quietly, Theta draws her pistol and inches towards the Prince. Up close, she admires his Zetsu, and wishes he were the type to use his talent for others, before pointing her gun at his head. She fires, expecting to follow shortly, and sees Tserriednich fall to the ground from the bullet shot. She sees Tserriednich land on a beautiful grassy landscape, and is stunned. Three minutes later, Theta snaps out of the vision, and sees herself back in Room 1004, but evidence of Tserriednich being shot missing. Guards barge into the room, and tell her to freeze. Theta raises her hands in surrender, but hears Tserriednich behind her telling the guards that they were just training. Tserriednich tells her to rest, and Theta looks up only to come face to face with Tserriednich's Guardian Spirit Beast, who gives her a warning not to lie again, before she passes out. 

Tserriednich's men tell him about their inability to move upon hearing the music, and Tserriednich decides to feel Melody out by judging her reaction to his men inviting her to him. He says to call her over and to make it sound like an invitation rather than a summons. 

Theta has a nightmare of a monstrous, wounded Tserriednich asking her if he can trust her, just like he asked on the first night. She sits up and sees Salkov watching over her, and learns that she was asleep for only fifteen minutes. He asks her to rest as he hands her a mirror to show an infection covering the left side of her face, spreading from the wound Tserriednich's Guardian Spirit Beast gave her. Salkov suggests that Tserriednich's Guardian Spirit Beast had marked her when she first lied, and now had given her a warning that may make her suffer a fate worse than death if unheeded. He guesses that it is giving those who lie so many chances because it plans to add them to the Prince's pawns by turning them into something inhuman. Theta and Salkov try to figure out what was real and what was an illusion. There was no blood when tested with luminol, yet Theta did fire her gun. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

On the ninth day, Cleapatro's investigator interviews Melody in the Justice Bureau on Tier 2 about her involvement in the Prince's escape plan. In Room 1009, Halkenburg and his men observe Sumidori's brainwaves and pulse and learn that his soul had possessed Shikaku's body. Halkenburg suggests four options for where Shikaku's soul is: 
Shikaku's soul is gone and he's dead. 
His soul is in Sumidori's body. 
The two coexist within Shikaku's body. 
It's somewhere (or in someone) else. 

Halkenburg tells them that his ability awakened after the confrontation with his father made him accept his fate, and shares his belief that it will help him win the succession battle, then shares his hypothesis and plan with his men. During Nen lessons in Room 1014, both Kurapika and Bill feel the rumbling of aura. Bill and Kurapika guess it was the Ninth, Seventh, or Fifth Prince judging by the direction and aura. In Room 1004, Salkov is surprised to hear that Tserriednich is applying the four major principles already. Tserriednich tells Salkov to measure the time he takes to enter Zetsu, and to spar with him when fast enough. He remarks to Salkov about a change of heart, now finding two-faced women cute. 

Theta and Tserriednich's exchange during the banquet replays from Tserriednich's perspective. Tserriednich immediately opens his eyes and jumps to the side. Theta does not react to him, and continues pointing the gun at where she sees him stand. Tserriednich is transfixed, but snaps ouf of entrancement after three minutes, and reels at what he just saw, fascinated by what Nen can do. Theta draw her gun and shoot him in the head. He quickly opens his eyes, darting to the side and falling to the floor. But he realizes that Theta will think he's still standing there and that she shot him. Tserriednich's guards come in and confront Theta, and Tserriednich decides to ask why she tried to kill him later when he sees her in shock. He has his guards invite Melody to him and ruminates on the new information he learned about his ability. Participants of Kurapika's Nen lessons wait for Ladiolus to return from the closed doors to judge the truth behind Kurapika's words. When Ladiolus steps out, Furykov inspects her, and concludes that she has truly learned Nen and is ready to train to control it, not controlled by Kurapika in some way. Kurapika requests the participants to not discuss what happened until all participants have completed, and invites Maor to be the next participant, who expected to go later owing to his status as a guard for a higher-ranked Prince. Maor steps into the master bedroom and sees Oito seated on the bed in front of Woble, and a table with two glasses with water and a single seed in each of them. Maor informs Tubeppa of his opinion that Kurapika's training is authentic, and recommends attending more lectures which would improve the chances of an alliance. Rihan stands guard and observes the exchange but occupies his mind with trying to understand Balsamilco's words and actions. 

Rihan thinks to Balsamilco's expression of hope for him to slay Halkenburg's Nen beast and wonders if he should be guarding Halkenburg instead of waiting for Tubeppa's beast to show itself. From the VVIP area, Balsamilco wishes for Rihan to understand his instructions, having intentionally avoided giving information to maximize Predator, and asked some questions he hopes Rihan thinks about as well. 

From her knowledge of Salé-salé's personality, Tubeppa concludes his absence from the concert was due to his death. She passes a thumb drive to Heisen with information for him to follow up on, and shifts her attention to identifying Salé-salé's cause of death without attracting suspicion to herself, concluding that the only way to proceed is to get Kurapika on her side. 

At 11:30 a.m. on the tenth day, Kurapika and Bill feel a fourth rumbling of aura, sooner than they expect it to come. 

At 10 a.m. on the ninth day. Kanjidol recounts what he saw in Room 1007 during the rumbling of aura. He reports the locations and actions of Luzurus and his guards within Room 1007, and mentions that the rumbling stopped after Shikaku shot himself, suggesting that Shikaku's suicide involved Halkenburg's ability somehow, and that it was likely that he was forced to commit suicide as part of a deal. At 11:30 a.m. on the tenth day, Benjamin and Balsamilco feel a fourth rumbling of aura, and hear Vict signal an emergency over the radio, saying something about Halkenburg, a bow, and invincibility before crying out and going silent. In Room 1003, Tenftory informs the Prince of the outcome of Kurapika's lessons and the changes he feels from being able to use Nen. Zhang Lei tries to understand his beast and its coins. He judges his own character and decides that it will likely be useless until he takes the throne, and decides to meet with Onior. Mafia War: Hunting the Heil-Ly

Hinrigh contacts Onior about the worsening situation with the Heil-Ly and their killing of several members from the Cha-R and their Xi-Yu.
