Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 391 

In Area E, Hinrigh is spotted from a distance by three Heil-Ly members: Padaille, Quorolle, and Tevelares. They make a loose plan to have Padaille go in first and make an opening for Quorolle and Tevelares to kill Hinrigh. Padaille uses Fistful of Weapons to attack Hinrigh from behind, but is caught by a set of Nen-reinforced handcuffs Hinrigh turned into pigeons with Biohazard. Quorolle and Tevelares shoot at the pigeons to no avail but draw Hinrigh's attention, and Padaille transforms his hand into a drill bit to escape his handcuffs and attack Hinrigh. Padaille manages to stab Hinrigh's hand, but he is kicked away and has his left hand handcuffed to his left ankle. Desperate, Padaille turns his free right hand into an axe, but Hinrigh catches it and kills Padaille with it. Quorolle and Tevelares abandon Padaille over fears of being captured and tortured into revealing the location of their hideout and decide to report back to Morena. 

A crowd gathers around Hinrigh and Padaille, and several military members approach them to manage the situation. Misha Hao appears and possesses Padaille using Zombie Girl, feigning normalcy and covering for Hinrigh. Corporal Maizan comes and lets "Padaille" walk away after confirming that Padaille is a civilian. Misha leaves and disposes of Padaille's corpse. Under the guise of warning Hinrigh, Maizan offers him information about Morena's location. After confirming Maizan's source, Hinrigh leaves to fetch Maizan's payment. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 392 

Zakuro uses Bloody Mary to spot passengers matching Hisoka's physique and Lynch confirms their identities with Body and Soul. They find a candidate, but Lynch is knocked out before she can report the man's identity to Zakuro. Intimidated by his aura, Zakuro explains the family's search for Hisoka and asks the man to follow him back to Hinrigh for a better explanation. 

Tsudonke speaks with a shopkeeper and learns of rumors of a fight between a Xi-Yu man (Hinrigh) and a member of the public (Padaille), and that Tier 1's general store could request items delivered to the Black Whale. He rejoins Ken'i and several other Cha-R family members in their search for Hisoka on Tier 3. They agree Hinrigh was involved and Ken'i decides to split the team up. Ken'i has some men search an area before reporting back to their office on Tier 5 and informing the Spiders, and has the rest continue searching for Hisoka on Tier 3 with him, telling them to let him handle Hisoka if found. 

In the Cha-R headquarters, a Cha-R member watches the security cameras as the Spiders idly wait for their assassin to return. Someone knocks and a Cha-R member gets up to open the door, expecting the return of their search team, but is stopped by the camera watchman. The Spiders see Luini's arm knocking on the door through a gap in the wall from his Nen space, and the watchman points out the search team returning. Phinks looks back and sees the other Cha-R members missing. The watchman comes to take a look as well, but is told by Nobunaga to bolt himself in the monitor room and let them know if anything happens to the search team. 

Luini slowly pulls into the base from his Nen space, and jokingly chastises the Spiders for betraying his expectations by working with the Cha-R instead of attacking them. He sees the Spiders annoyed and rapidly backtracks, proposing to work together with the Spiders to destroy the world. Nobunaga quickly stabs him through the forehead. The search team enter to find their underlings missing, and Nobunaga explains that Luini had killed them, pointing to Luini's dismembered body right behind them. Nobunaga declares their intent to crush the Heil-Ly while they look for Hisoka, and Phinks and Feitan thank the Cha-R for their hospitality and make plans to return in the future. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 

Back at the Heil-Ly secret hideout, several Heil-Ly members discuss how to proceed with the loss of Luini, while standing in a closed room around Luini's severed lower half and several corpses. Daemon, Gelato, Perigord, Bille, and Matvere debate the best way to continue leveling up without Luini. Voconte proposes using his Nen ability as an alternative, but mentions that they would have to wait for victims instead of finding them. Quorolle and Televeras enter and ask about the possibility of anyone being an emitter to defeat Hinrigh. Gelato, Quorolle, and Televeras head to Morena, and ask for her opinion on how to deal with Hinrigh. Morena highlights many factors to consider and offers to let Gelato know her type. Gelato declines, preferring to be surprised. 

In a dumpster, Zakuro, regains consciousness and sees Lynch, who asks if he had spoken with Hisoka after she was knocked out. They decide to let Hinrigh know that Hisoka was on his way to see a movie. After bringing Hinrigh to the cineplex, Zakuro is sent away to avoid his presence being interpreting as revenge, and leaves to help Lynch find Chrollo. Hinrigh sees Hisoka and shares with him that the Cha-R is working with the Phantom Troupe and the Xi-Yu to target the Heil-Ly, then offers him a room in the VVIP area on Tier 1 so that their Phantom Troupe allies might not find him and force them to attack him. Hinrigh makes it clear that neither mafia family intends on interfering between him and the Troupe, and his offer is accepted. 

Ken'i finds Corporal Maizan waiting for Hinrigh to return with money, who offers to cut Ken'i into the deal. Xi-Yu lieutenant Connelly returns with the money for Maizan, and after Hinrigh returns, Maizan brings them and several underlings to the Tier 3 first-class cabins. Maizan knocks on Room 3101's door and is greeted by Gateaume. Maizan asks Gateaume to sit on his bed and keep his hands up as he enters. Hinrigh and Ken'i wait a moment, but notice Maizan has vanished, and ask Gateaume where Maizan went. He feigns ignorance and invites them in. While outside the room, Hinrigh throws a knife at Gateaume to threaten him and check for a trap. Hinrigh asks Gateaume to step toward them but Gateaume insists on using the bathroom, so Hinrigh throws a knife into Gateaume's leg. Gateaume screams and falls to the ground, but Hinrigh notes the lack of blood and calls him out for faking his injury. Gateaume gives up the act, vanishing into Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 394 

Ken'i proposes leaving the Heil-Ly to the Phantom Troupe. Hinrigh tries to stop his underling Tassi from retrieve his knife, but is too late and Tassi vanishes as he steps into the room, confirming the trap. Hinrigh speculates that this was how Luini abducted the other mafia members, but Ken'i explains that Nobunaga had killed Luini and that the Spiders plan to crush the Heil-Ly. Ken'i walks off and Hinrigh decides to return to the general passenger area to retrieve the cat-camcorder and review its footage. 

Tassi teleports into the Heil-Ly base and is confused by the change in his environment. Before he can react, he is killed by Bille and his body is disposed of. Matvere, Sodom, Orarge, and Yokotani report to Morena that their liaison with Tserriednich had gone silent. Suspecting that Tserriednich might be aware of them, Morena considers Gateaume's report of Room 3101 being discovered and has Sodom work with Dogman to abduct one of Tserriednich's soldiers for her to infect with Contagion to track his movements. She has her men use an alternate entrance and instructs Orarge and Yokotani to watch Room 3101's door, but expect only Mafia and Soldiers. Notre and Soufflé are instructed to return to Room 3131 and report on the situation in Room 3101. Morena and Dogman agree to prioritize him at the new disposal plant to quickly get him to Level 50. 

A group of Tserriednich's soldiers (and former classmates in the Kakin Military School) storm a Heil-Ly hideout while looking for Morena and find it abandoned. They discover the corpse of the missing Cha-R warehouse guard, giving them justification for the forced entry. While resuming patrol, Otocin suggests the possibility that the Heil-Ly has Nen users to his Corporal Gipper, having learned the information from Borksen, a member of the Royal Quarters Security Detail. Gipper dismisses Otocin's suggestions. The other soldiers note the corpse being a few days old and agree that the police and army are likely to overlook the matter, but worry about the threat of war between Kakin and its mafia if the Hunter Association get involved and suggests Kakin eliminate its mafia to protect the Princes. They worry about the Heil-Ly's intent to upset the balance, and about the threat of the Mafia families colluding with Tiers 4 and 5's citizens, outnumbering the military. Momolly theorizes that Tserriednich's lack of action is due to him learning Nen, and the group make contingency plans in case the order is sent to avoid the Heil-Ly, so they can avoid work. Borksen meets up with the group and confirms Tserriednich's learning of Nen. She mentions that Theta and Salkov started acting strangely after Kurapika's emergency broadcast about Nen, and the group agree to avoid Morena. The group agree to reach out to either the Xi-Yu or Cha-R families. 

Hinrigh sees Perigord and Bille on the cat-camcorder's recording and confirms that the Heil-Ly are teleporting. He rejoins Ken'i and the other Mafia members, and review their information. Ken'i shares that the Spiders were headed to deal with the trap. Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan investigate the trap by cutting through the back of Room 3102 to get to Room 3101's bathroom via a construction hallway. The spiders discover signs of recent inhabitation in the construction hallway. While observing the situation, Nobunaga theorizes that the Heil-Ly are a lot like the Phantom Troupe, where the loss of a companion has a big impact on them. He has a flashback to a time Meteor City before the Phantom Troupe existed. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 395 

Running through mountains of rubbish, a young Franklin catches a cassette tape, and together with a young Shalnark and young Chrollo, runs away from a young Uvogin, having been snitched on by a young Machi. Uvogin throws a rock and hits Shalnark and Franklin holds Uvogin off while Chrollo escapes with the cassette tape. A young Phinks and young Feitan cut him off with a motorcycle, and steal the cassette tape from him before driving off, but Chrollo pulls out the actual cassette tape, having surrendered a fake. At the local church, Lisores, a Meteor City elder welcomes Chrollo back, but warns him against visiting the city outskirts alone due to the raiding parties in the inner-city neighborhoods. Chrollo takes the cassette tape to the screening room, where he finds a young Pakunoda. The two try to watch it but are greeted by static. Lisores reports to the Meteor City elders, and suggests Chrollo's opinions may interest them. 

The next day, Chrollo shows the cassette tape recording of the Clean-Up Rangers in a foreign language to Pakunoda, having simply forwarded through the static previously seen. Pakunoda excitedly suggests calling the others, but is stopped by Chrollo who suggests dubbing the recording so they can all understand. Pakunoda calls Sheila and Sarasa over to help record the dubbing, and return to find Chrollo already finished with the translation. As Meteor City residents get abducted in another part of the city, the four begin recording their dubbing. 

A few days later, an announcement is heard of a special screening for the church's regular assembly. Chrollo helps Linores distribute snacks to the children when Uvogin comes and confronts him for stealing the recording and showing it, telling him to meet him after the screening. Phinks and Feitan come by to mock Chrollo and invite him to play soccer after his meeting with Uvogin, and are convinced to help give out snacks by Sarasa. During the screening, the children are amazed to hear the show in their language, but Shalnark recognizes the voices of Chrollo and Pakunoda. Suddenly, the audio cuts off, and Chrollo quickly begins a live dubbing, pretending that the audio was delivered live from the very beginning. The audience realizes that Chrollo has been voicing all the male characters, and Chrollo resumes the dubbing with Pakunoda, Sheila, and Sarasa, entrancing the audience with his intensity and realism. After the screening, Chrollo and his friends see Uvogin waiting for him, ready to confront him for stealing the tape, but Sarasa "dubs" Uvogin and gives Chrollo his approval, denying Uvogin the chance to pretend otherwise. Uvogin, Nobunaga, Feitan, Phinks, Shalnark, and Franklin ask to be in the next recording, and Uvogin gives Chrollo his real approval, shaking his hand. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 396 

While searching for more people to dub the recordings, the group asks Machi to voice a character, but she refuses. Pakunoda suggests that they're more of a troupe than a group. Uvogin expresses his desire to perform everywhere, to be the world's greatest villain. Chrollo notices Sarasa missing, but Phinks dismisses Chrollo's fears, explaining that it was Sarasa's turn to sort the trash. Unbeknownst to them, Sarasa was running off by herself, to search for more tapes to surprise them. 

Sarasa is uncharacteristically late for the next showing, so Sheila brings Chrollo to Sarasa's wet nurses in search of her. The Troupe search for her to no avail, and reconvene before the showing begins. Chrollo steps onto the stage, and apologizes to the gathered children, explaining and taking responsibility for Sarasa's disappearance and the show's cancellation as a result. The crowd eagerly volunteers to help, cheered on by Uvogin and Phinks, and Chrollo is moved to tears. Having searched through the city, the Troupe decide to search the city's outskirts, and find Sarasa's pendant on a trail to a forest. In the forest, they see a black garbage bag hanging from a tree, and a note pinned to a tree. Uvogin asks Chrollo to read the note, unable to read it himself, and cuts the bag down for Chrollo to catch. Chrollo opens the bag, to find Sarasa's dismembered body with another note nailed to her forehead. Chrollo refuses to reveal the notes' contents, and the troupe bring Sarasa's body back to mourn. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 397 

At Sarasa's funeral, Machi is impressed by how well Sarasa is presented, and Lisores explains that Renko had embalmed her. Uvogin leaves the cassette tape with Sarasa. Machi asks Renko to teach her embalming, and Renko agrees after realizing Machi can see her aura, leaving her directions to meet her in Kirimori Valley. 

One day when Chrollo and Uvogin visit Sarasa's grave, Chrollo asks Uvogin if he would wait for him instead of immediately setting out to find Sarasa's killers. Chrollo convinces Uvogin to wait three years by sharing his plan to turn Meteor City into a haven for criminals, including those that expose themselves during the upcoming communication revolution across the world by sharing images of their crimes, like Sarasa's killers, who Chrollo believed had recorded images by the theatrical location of where Sarasa's corpse was found and the signs that someone had been recording. The rest of the Troupe steps out of the forest and joins them, except Sheila, who quietly leaves. Chrollo explains his desire to become a villain that the whole world fears so that no criminal would approach Meteor City. Chrollo asks Uvogin to lead the Troupe, but Uvogin refuses, insisting that Chrollo be the leader, and promises to follow Chrollo till his death. The flashback ends when the Troupe reconvenes several years later, now as the Spiders. 

The spiders use 3102's occupant to test Room 3101's trap, and their test subject finally vanishes when he steps into Room 3101 through its main door. After Feitan confirms the trap's workings with another room's occupant, he is approached by Hinrigh and Ken'i as he prepares to enter the trap. Hinrigh proposes bringing along a homing device with them before they do, using Biohazard to transform it into a raw oyster. Hinrigh swallows it and hands the receiver to Phinks before stepping into the trap himself. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 398 

After being teleported to the same position Tassi had been, Hinrigh quickly turns and drops to the ground in anticipation of attack. He sees a seemingly-abandoned base and plans his next course of action, but senses a malicious aura behind him and jumps forward. He turns around to see Nobunaga behind him, having chosen to assist Hinrigh. Nobunaga confirms that they are in the hideout by showing that the walls were reinforced by Nen, able to resist his slashes. They open doors and see signs of recent presence. Nobunaga realizes that their methods of testing the trap with Room 3101's neighbors might have given the Heil-Ly a heads up. Hinrigh opens the most suspicious door and reveals eight Heil-Ly members sitting in a lounge room, conversing as though Hinrigh and Nobunaga were not there. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 399 

Hesitant about another doorway trap, Hinrigh throws a knife at Terebellum's head, but he uses Sweet Home to transfer the blade out through his left hand into the drink can he is holding. Terebellum stands up to face Hinrigh, but Soufflé grabs him, reminding him of their orders not to fight. Hinrigh throws another knife at Terebellum, who dodges it as he pushes Soufflé away. Perigord, now in the knife's trajectory, raises his hand to experiment with his Nen, but Orarge blocks the knife with a book, asking him to be more careful as the 'Organ'. Yokotani stands up, far enough from the door to avoid any slashes from Nobunaga, and introduces himself. He is caught off guard when Nobunaga unexpectedly throws his katana through his forehead, but saved by Terebellum's Sweet Home. Yokotani uses LSDF to conjure Nen puppets, which capture Nobunaga and force him through the doorway, teleporting him back to Room 3101. Hinrigh quickly runs away to regurgitate and hide the oyster-tracker, managing to do so before being forced through himself. Having run out of resources for the day, Hinrigh decides to take a break, and Nobunaga finds Feitan and Phinks to follow the transmitter. 

Nobunaga rejoins Feitan and Phinks as they debate whether the hideout is on Tier 2 or Tier 4. They head to the passageway between Tiers 3 and 4 and are let through without resistance by the guards, having received notice from the Cha-R and Xi-Yu families to let them through. As they descend, Feitan notices that the transmitter is getting further from them, and concludes that the Heil-Ly base is on Tier 2. Nobunaga realizes the source of his uneasiness — the Heil-Ly are instigating a fight without 'just cause', breaking the mafia's code of conduct. They once again debate whether to find Franklin before continuing their search for the Heil-Ly. 

In Room 1006, Izunavi observes Giuliano's change in behavior after reading Tyson's book. He theorizes that Tyson's Guardian Spirit Beast's power is linked to Tyson's book and propagates devotion. He guesses that it might be possible to get Nasubi to read it and call off the war, and asks if Tyson has given copies of his book to other Princes. Tyson reveals that her mother Queen Katrono had been the one to give copies of the book to the other Queens and Princes as Tyson's condition for joining the succession war. Izunavi encourages Tyson to get Katrono to get Nasubi to read the book. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 

Guards stand outside Room 1010 and prevent military from entering, while inside Seiko demands to see Fugetsu. She fights to keep Room 1010 from being cordoned off and demands no one but her talk to Fugetsu, demanding that anyone that wants to do so go through her. She racks her brains to think of a way to convince the others that Kacho is alive and to make up for the fact that Fugetsu is not strong enough to win the war, consoled by the fact that Fugetsu remains protected at the Justice Bureau for now. 

In one of the Justice Department's interrogation rooms, the gravity of Melody's actions starts to hit her when Without You in the form of Kacho (hereon referred to as "Kacho") suggests that they might be reliant on Fugetsu's aura, an additional burden. "Kacho" plans to make Fugetsu the victor by capitalizing on her presumed death to surprise others, but does not want to yet reveal her death to Fugetsu. Kaiser, Cleapatro's investigator for Room 1010, agrees with "Kacho" and encourages Melody to help her. Kaiser reveals that Zhang Lei and Tserriednich have invited her to perform in their quarters, and Benjamin, Tubeppa, and Luzurus have offered pardons in exchange for an encore, careful not to mention Keeney's death around "Kacho". Kaiser says that these Nen-aware Princes have caught on to Melody's involvement in Kacho's escape and are trying to get Melody's help for their schemes. Lowering the amount of Princes sounds ideal to "Kacho", but Kaiser points out the risk of acting while many higher-ranking Princes remain, risking mistakes by acting without knowing who targets who. Kaiser proposes fooling all the Princes who made requests of Melody and removing them by poisoning them while unconscious. Melody wonders why Kaiser would help them, and Kaiser confesses his infatuation with Melody. Melody hears his heartbeat contradict his words and wonders about the possibility of him being manipulated and having a hidden benefactor. "Kacho" leaves through the walls, and then Kaiser joins her to keep up guises before letting her return to the Prince's residential area. 

Kaiser calls Steiner over and hands him a memory stick containing a contingency plan in case martial law is declared. "Kacho" makes her way to Fugetsu's room in the Justice Bureau and discovers Fugetsu missing. She pulls the blanket from Fugetsu's bed away to reveal Magical Worm's door. Right as she wonders where Fugetsu went, Fugetsu pops up through the door, uncharacteristically pale and delirious. Fugetsu tells "Kacho" that she can now use Magical Worm by herself multiple times a day, and expresses feeling overflowing with power. "Kacho" convinces her to rest and lets Melody know about Fugetsu's state, who contacts Kaiser to organize a situation where Melody can hear Fugetsu's heartbeat. On her way to a meeting with Zhang Lei, Melody walks past Fugetsu in a state of near-Zetsu, sees evil spirits hovering around her, clinging to her body, and decides that Fugetsu needs an exorcist. 

Zhang Lei compliments Melody's performance, and asks her for two requests – to perform again, and to keep a coin from his Guardian Spirit Beast. Representing Tserriednich, Vantaine invites Melody to Room 1004. Melody declines but expresses her willingness to perform anywhere under the right circumstances. 

In her next meeting with Kaiser, Melody informs him about Fugetsu's condition and suggests holding off on the plan. Melody asks for herself to be bailed out, but Kaiser refuses, explaining that it would mean their inability to refuse a Prince's requests. Melody asks Kaiser to inform Kurapika of their findings and wonders who is behind her situation, unable to identify someone responsible for Kaiser's possible manipulation. 

At 11:45 a.m. during Kurapika's Nen lesson on the tenth day, Longhi is invited into Room 1014's master bedroom to have her Nen awakened. Furykov watches and wonders how the final Nen user who he noticed concealing their proficiency with Nen would act. After Kurapika shuts the doors, Longhi reveals that she can already use Nen, surprising Kurapika and Bill, who react in preparation to defend themselves. Longhi activates her Moonlight Act to simultaneously prove that she was not responsible for the deaths of Barrigen and Myuhan, and to get Kurapika's assistance. Kurapika activates his Dowsing Chain and prepares to shoot Longhi if he finds her lying. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 

Longhi explains that Moonlight Act can reward or penalize people who fulfil the obligations of a voluntarily-agreed-to contract with her after she explain her terms to them. She says that she wishes to give him additional power to further a personal goal of hers. She reveals that she is one of many children fathered by Beyond, raised in sham marriages arranged by Beyond and set on the path to become Royal Guards, many likely successful given that they have had Nen since birth. She explains her initial assumption that Beyond's goal was to place his children in important positions close to the next king of Kakin, and reveals that their ability to use Nen since their birth was a side effect of Beyond's Nen attack. Longhi learnt about this when her half-sister Makaha became suspicious of her own parents, and was motivated to investigate her situation secretly, discovering Longhi while Longhi herself was serving Duazul in the process. After the Seed Urn Ceremony, they had noticed a mark they had in common, and consulted a renowned Nen user The Viz translations describe the person as an "exorcist" despite the normal term for exorcist not being used in the original Japanese. who explained that the curse placed on them would activate and curse an unknown target to death upon their own. 

Despite a reminder from Bill that taking too long would draw suspicion, Kurapika lets Longhi continue to help her fulfil the conditions for activating Moonlight Act. Longhi says that Beyond's lack of movement despite Makaha's failure to join Camilla's entourage was an indication that their targets were not simply the Princes they were assigned to, though she is sure that it is a Prince. Though unsure of who their targets are, Longhi is confident that Beyond is trying to influence the succession battle. Amidst Bill and Kurapika's speculation about Beyond and his curse, Longhi shares her hypothesis that one of Kakin's fourteen Princes is Beyond's child. At Kurapika's request, Oito confirms that the rules do not specify that a participant must have Nasubi as their biological father. 

Sakata knocks on the door to ask if anything is wrong. Kurapika steps out and explains that they were negotiating a truce, and would explain more afterwards. Furykov notes no change in Kurapika's aura and wonders what they were discussing. Babimyna watches in silence. Kurapika returns and reactivates Dowsing Chain before asking Longhi to continue. Longhi explains the details of the proposed contract between Tubeppa and Woble that would last until 9 a.m. on Sunday, the fifteenth day, and renewed if Tubeppa had a representative in Room 1014 then, that neither side would act maliciously against the other side's Prince, Queen, or Guards, and Kurapika violating the rules will force him into Zetsu for one week as penalty, though Longhi promises to not arbitrarily interpret Kurapika's actions as malicious. 

Longhi adds another stipulation for her true plan: 
Woble's side must identify the Prince that is Beyond's child before either Tubeppa withdraws, or before only two Princes remain. 
If it is discovered that none of the Princes are Beyond's child, the ability would immediately activate and reward Woble's side. 

Longhi promises to write Kurapika an identical contract if he is willing to continue the investigation in the event that their truce is broken before that. Kurapika notes that Dowsing Chain has not indicated that Longhi is lying. Longhi asks Kurapika to listen to her current true plan before signing—should Beyond's child be among the Princes, to kill them herself. 

Kurapika agrees to Longhi's contract and to collaborate with Tubeppa. The participants watch the three step out. Furykov sees no signs of a Nen battle but that Longhi looked like she was crying and wonders what happened. He feels something slightly different in Kurapika's aura but is unable to identify what. Furykov wonders if his deduction that Longhi was the assassin was incorrect. Kurapika invites Tenftory in, the last to have his Nen awakened that day, and promises to discuss the matter with the participants afterwards. 

At around 2:00 p.m. on the tenth day, Beyond asks Kanzai to arrange for someone to meet him. 

In Room 1003, at 1:30 p.m. on the tenth day, Zhang Lei learns from Tenftory that Tubeppa and Woble would defer the throne to him if they were the final three Princes remaining, though in return, Woble had requested Sakata and Hashito to extend their support indefinitely. Coventoba stands guard nearby and watches their interaction. Zhang Lei rewards Tenftory with his second coin, and tells him that holding on to them would bring him fortune. He thinks that they might begin to show their power only when held by others. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 

In Room 1005, at roughly the same time, Tubeppa learns from Maor and Longhi that Kurapika had agreed to their alliance, on condition that Woble and Oito accompany him when he comes to Room 1005. Maor adds that Zhang Lei's personal guards also demanded to attend, in the interest of sharing information. Tubeppa welcomes the idea. Rihan eavesdrops while standing guard and learns of Tubeppa and Kurapika's collusion. Tubeppa guesses from Tserriednich's absence from the banquet on Day 8 that working with her is unimportant to him. She reminds herself not to be choosy and hopes Benjamin directs his attention toward Zhang Lei instead of her. Tubeppa tells Maor and Longhi to impress their closeness with Zhang Lei upon Benjamin's soldiers at the next class. Suddenly, Rihan notices Tubeppa's Guardian Spirit Beast suddenly appear. He wonders if the timing of its appearance was significant, which would imply that it is a conditional type. 

Somewhere alone, Balsamilco tests a gas emission contraption in his shoe. He takes a potent pathological weapon out of his pocket, loads it into his shoe, and resolves to erase Halkenburg with it. 

In Room 1004, at 8:00 a.m. on the eleventh day, Salkov announces to Tserriednich that he has broken the 10-second barrier to activate his Zetsu. Tserriednich is unsatisfied with the result and tells Salkov that they will try again in 10 seconds while continuing to multitask. Salkov echoes Theta's previous sentiments that Tserriednich would use Nen selfishly. He concludes that the only explanation for Theta's testimony was that Tserriednich had activated some ability during Zetsu, making him an extraordinary specialist. Salkov identifies the possibility that his jester-like Nen beast stores aura in it under normal conditions and then activates its power when Zetsu is used. He also considers the possibility that Theta was manipulated by his Guardian Spirit Beast to give false testimony. Vantine enters to report that the Ministry of Justice and Benjamin had suspected Melody's involvement in the escape attempt and are continuing to detain her and station soldiers there respectively. Tserriednich tells Vantine not to let Benjamin get ahead of him and instructs him to continue the meetings to win Melody over. 

In Melody's cell at the Justice Bureau at 6:00 a.m. on the eleventh day, "Kacho" shows Melody a picture of a mark on Fugetsu's back that was not there before their escape attempt. Melody says that it looks like an injection mark, but "Kacho" dismisses the possibility since they had been immediately taken into custody by the Ministry of Justice after they returning to their room. Melody suggests that it is a mark caused by Nen, from a trap Fugetsu had fallen into. She asks if Fugetsu had said anything strange yesterday, and "Kacho" thinks back to Fugetsu's comment about using Magical Worm many times in a day. Melody tells "Kacho" to persuade Fugetsu not to use the ability anymore and suggests that Fugetsu's recent behavior shows signs of addiction. Melody shares that she was wrong in thinking that one of the Princes who had met her was using Fugetsu as part of a negotiation. She says that if Fugetsu continues using the enemy's ability, she will not last until next weekend. Melody suggests identifying and stopping the culprit, though it would be difficult to identify them without any clues. "Kacho" has an idea of who the culprit could be. 

Later, Kaiser joins them and they continue their discussion in his office. "Kacho" suggests that Luzurus is responsible since he sells drugs to help back the Cha-R Family and guesses that his Guardian Spirit Beast is responsible for Fugetsu's condition. Melody points out that even if Luzurus was behind it, he did not likely consciously attack Fugetsu, so he would not be easy to persuade. "Kacho" decides that the only way to confirm and stop the ability is to defeat Luzurus or his Nen beast, deciding that they must take action immediately. "Kacho" shares her plan and that Fugetsu could connect to the inside of the first lifeboat in the emergency area though they had been unable to test if someone other than the twins could enter the door, and speculates that others could come and go if they were not inside. 

Melody says that she would need someone to carry an unconscious Luzurus through the tunnel to the lifeboat. Kaiser volunteers to be their mule and tells them when to act to have an alibi. Kaiser reasons out some obvious pitfalls but proposes solutions for them. 

"Kacho" asks Melody about her thoughts on the plan. Melody asks how Fugetsu would connect her door to Luzurus' master bedroom, because she needs to have visited somewhere before she could visit it with Magical Worm. Kaiser volunteers to get Fugetsu access to the necessary locations. He asks them to devise an excuse for Fugetsu to personally meet each Prince, pointing out their chance to increase the number of locations Fugetsu could travel to. "Kacho" proposes to write a "last message" to be delivered to each Prince. Kaiser agrees and tells "Kacho" to notify Fugetsu, and looks up to see Melody and "Kacho" looking at him. Believing that they still had doubts about his willingness to get involved, he prepares to restate how he feels about Melody. 

Melody tells Kaiser there is no turning back, and asks him if he is sure he wants to risk everything. Kaiser confirms that he does, adding that he thinks it would be best if Fugetsu takes the throne and changes the system. He shares that he did not join the Ministry of Justice just to blindly uphold the law, he also wants to get the world closer to his ideal world. As Kaiser speaks, Melody notes his heartbeat is unchanged. She prepares to assume that Kaiser is on the enemy's side, and to protect Fugetsu, decides to maintain the relationship for the time being. 

Kaiser escorts Fugetsu around Tier 1, with a group of armed soldiers. They first head to the VVIP area to pass Kacho's letter to Benjamin. While seated, Benjamin observes the spirits clinging to Fugetsu. As Fugetsu approaches Benjamin, his Guardian Spirit Beast dispels the spirits around her. Benjamin notices them vanish, confirming to him that they are low-level evil spirits plaguing those in a weakened state. He decides that she had fallen into despair after losing her sister, believing she would not last ten more days. 

Benjamin opens the letter and tells the witnesses that it contains nothing worth sharing before quickly changing the topic to Musse's location. Kaiser tells him that Musse has not yet been found, offers to force thorough searches of all the Princes' quarters, and trails off to let Benjamin cut him off. Benjamin says that it would likely be pointless because of the ongoing court case to determine whether royal privilege or military authority had precedence, but regardless of the outcome, their situation was not dire enough to need martial law. Kaiser agrees, and guesses that Benjamin mentioning martial law was a sign that he was planning to implement it, a likely slip of the tongue while trying to not react to the contents of the letter. 

Kaiser's group make their way to Tubeppa and Tyson so Fugetsu can pass them Kacho's final letter, then to Room 1007, where Luzurus thanks the Ministry of Justice for vouching that his quarters are drug-free in exchange for meeting Fugetsu. Luzurus apologizes to Fugetsu for Kacho before reading the letter, watched by Kaiser and several of Luzurus' guards. Luzurus says that its contents are surprising and hears Fugetsu confirm that she did not know its contents or if Kacho written the same message to all the Princes. Luzurus guesses that Kacho would not have wanted to get her little sister involved and tells Kaiser that the Ministry of Justice might want to follow up on its contents. Kaiser takes the letter from Luzurus and pretends to read it for the first time. 

Kaiser thinks back to an earlier discussion with "Kacho" and Melody where he suggested dropping information bombs on the other Princes through letters "Kacho" would write containing insider information that the Ministry of Justice had gathered. Melody expresses uncertainty that anyone would believe that Kacho would have known such information. Kaiser dismisses it saying that each Prince would be too desperate to discover the source of the leak to think of it. He reassures "Kacho" and Melody that he will deflect questions that dig too deep into Kacho, and that he had no intention of having her return to Tier 1 until the succession contest is finished. 

Kaiser interprets Luzurus letting him read the letter as a possible sign that he is cautious. Kaiser suggests the letter contains rumors. Luzurus suggests that it might have been a tactic to get the Ministry of Justice to investigate each Prince and expose actual wrongdoing. Luzurus thinks about his off-feeling about the whole situation. He concludes that the contents of the letters to each Prince were different and that if Kacho had intended to pit him against Tserriednich while having Benjamin protect Fugetsu, acting carelessly would be dangerous. Luzurus decides to say that he has no idea how true the letter is and has no desire to find out either, and tells Kaiser to keep the letter. As they leave Room 1007's entrance hallway, Basho gives Fugetsu a good luck charm that temporarily keeps the low-level spirits away and hopes Melody can help her before her situation gets worse. Kaiser bows to Basho. 

Later, at 8:50 a.m. on the eleventh day in Kaiser's office, "Kacho" suggests acting quickly in case Luzurus is not the culprit. She says that they would be questioned in the infirmary while Fugetsu was treated, and stationing guards and Justice Bureau officials outside the room would give them perfect alibis. Melody agrees with the plan, stating that they could not confirm who the culprit was, though Basho had managed to give them some time. Melody shares that she would use the ocarina, but it would not change the fact that she could not do anything else while using her ability. Kaiser reassures them that it would be fine even if she could not do anything, that if the tunnel were about 10 meters long, he could carry Luzurus himself and still make it in time. 

Melody brings up a rumbling that happened right above the Ministry of Justice and guesses that the rumbling belonged to the ability of Halkenburg or his Guardian Spirit Beast. Since Halkenburg himself had fallen ill, Melody asks to know as many details about whether it was planned or not since it was crucial to know. She also reminds Kaiser that she must hand him the letter when he wakes up. Kaiser also reassures her that this is under control since he had assigned someone he knows to Halkenburg's attendant duty. Melody hopes Halkenburg can put his letter to good use, containing information about Benjamin's secrets, Tserriednich's depravity, and the truth of Halkenburg's own birth. She also hopes that Kurapika could devise a plan so no more Princes had to die using the information delivered to him via Fugetsu's letter. 

At 6:45 a.m. on the eleventh day, Balsamilco turns up at the Ministry of Justice Prosecution and Courthouse Area in preparation for his trial with Halkenburg. He mentally runs through his plans, confirming that Halkenburg had no guards around him to activate his ability with, and that his use of TSK-17 would have no risk of killing anyone other than Halkenburg. After he clears the security scans, he walks around, and eventually turns into a narrow corridor, where he senses something is off and stops. He turns around to see Vict staring at him. Balsamilco is shocked to see Vict, who begins gesturing with his hands, communicating using military sign language that he had discovered the secret to Halkenburg's ability. Around the corner, outside Balsamilco's vision, Halkenburg activates The Boy Who Shoots the Arrow: Grimmal the Dissonance, powers it with a group of civilian supporters, aims at Balsamilco through walls, and releases his bow. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Through a call, Benjamin hears that the trial between Balsamilco and Halkenburg has not started; Balsamilco's request for postponement due to equipment issues was rejected, but Halkenburg's request for postponement due to illness was accepted. Benjamin wonders if Balsamilco was trying to communicate to him that the mission was not executed, but dismisses that because Halkenburg's ability had been activated, and guesses that Balsamilco was the target though wondering why Halkenburg himself had fallen ill. Benjamin hears that Halkenburg was surrounded by roughly a dozen supporters, and guesses that Halkenburg could activate his ability with any group of supporters. He hears that Balsamilco is asking to speak with him on the phone, and is immediately wary of a trap. 

Balsamilco tells Benjamin that he had not executed his mission yet, that Halkenburg had attempted to shoot him, missed, and collapsed, due to overexertion or not having guards powering his ability. Benjamin remains wary of Balsamilco and prepares to activate martial law if he returned to Tier 1 without completing his mission, suspecting a coup d'état planned with Halkenburg. Balsamilco acknowledges the suspicious position he is in, but asks for permission to meet Halkenburg, and promises not to return until he can prove that he has finished his mission when Benjamin allows him to. The call ends and Benjamin summons Butch to inform him of what to do if Balsamilco returned, before hearing from another soldier that Fugetsu and Kaiser were there to pass him a letter. 

In Room 01, Unma receives a call from Halkenburg, who tells her of his plans and that he would not see her at the coming Sunday banquet. Unma expresses sorrow at not being able to see him there, but tells her son Halkenburg how proud of him she is. 

Benjamin receives Kacho's letter from Fugetsu, where Kacho describes internet theories claiming that Unma had asked Duazul a favor in order to protect Halkenburg from assassination plots by his older siblings, before threatening to reveal more secrets, and asking Benjamin to protect Fugetsu. Benjamin dismisses its contents as online rumors, and tells Kaiser that the letter contained nothing worth showing. As Fugetsu and Kaiser leave, Benjamin decides that Kacho was simply bluffing with her threats, since she did not have enough influence to dig up dirt that he could not. Zhang Lei, Camilla, Tubeppa, and Tyson are seen reading their letters. While Fugetsu rests in the infirmary, "Kacho", Melody, and Kaiser discuss how Tserriednich, Marayam, and Halkenburg were the only Princes to not accept a letter, though Halkenburg not getting one was because he is sick. They agree to carry out their plan against Luzurus at the night of the next day. 

In Room 1014, Kurapika explains to Oito that his letter implied that physical escape from the ship resulted in death. He asks permission from Oito to do something to protect Woble and the other younger Princes. In Room 1003, Zhang Lei looks at his coins and has his guards theorize what they could do. Their suggestions do not impress him, and he asks them to call Room 1014. 

While walking, Kaiser and Steiner discuss the mass syncope episode that Halkenburg had warned them about. Steiner mentions that Worio Bay had fallen asleep in a same manner but at a different time and place than the others, and Kaiser goes to visit him. When Kaiser visits Wario, Wario shows him the mark on the back of his hand, and tells Kaiser that Halkenburg is going to die soon. In Benjamin's room in the VVIP area, Benjamin hears Balsamilco report over the phone that the mission has been completed. Unbeknownst to him, Halkenburg's soul possesses Balsamilco's body. The twelve supporters that helped Halkenburg earlier stand around Halkenburg as he lies unconscious in bed. Kaiser steps into the room and observes from a distance. 

In Room 1014, Kurapika tells Oito that he wished to make her letter public, proposing to tell the Princes that its contents would be made known during the next round of lessons, which began the next day. Kurapika turns up with Oito and Woble at Room 1003 at Zhang Lei's request, and after some experimentation, gives Zhang Lei his guess at what the coins produced by his Guardian Spirit Beast can do. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

Kurapika suggests that Zhang Lei's Guardian Spirit Beast has a cumulative conjuration ability, which allows the coins produced by his Guardian Spirit Beast to accumulate Nen over time, increasing by a factor of ten every ten days. Eventually, it could open the aura nodes of its owner, and after more time possibly allow the owner to manifest a Nen ability. Due to Coventoba's presence, Kurapika chooses not to mention the possibility that the coin could induce semi-forced loyalty to Zhang Lei through pseudo-coercive manipulation. Zhang Lei thanks Kurapika for his explanation, and gives him the coin that appeared that day. Kurapika thanks him and tells Zhang Lei that their other matter would need more time, and that the second round of Nen classes should be key. Zhang Lei advises Kurapika that the others likely used the first round of Nen classes to gather intel and lay their groundwork, and he should prepare himself. As Kurapika leaves, he thinks about the balance of the Kakin mafia's three families and how it placed them in a more precarious situation, since if any of them died, Benjamin would have an excuse to impose martial law, which would give him the authority to force the Princes into house arrest for their safety where his guards could freely do his bidding. 

Cheadle leads a team of doctors and rushes Halkenburg's body to the general hospital on Tier 3 after he collapsed and became unresponsive. "Balsamilco" watches as Halkenburg's body is rushed away and reports the matter to Benjamin, who congratulates him on his good work and lets him know that he would send the royal and military medical teams to supervise Halkenburg's body and prevent interference. Halkenburg in Balsamilco's body thinks about his ability and prepares to take a sleeping pill right before his original body dies, so that he can control Balsamilco's body for roughly ten hours after his own body's death. 

The Princes pass the remainder of the eleventh day in their own ways. Benjamin and Furykov read a tablet as they wait for an update from Balsamilco. Tserriednich manages to reduce the time it takes for him to activate Zetsu down to below 3.5 seconds and resolves to continue carving the time down. "Kacho" sits with Fugetsu as she rests and watches over her, her own body starting to become more translucent. In Room 1014, Kurapika lets the guards of other Princes in his room know that the second round of Nen lessons would begin the next day at 9:00 a.m. He asks them their thoughts on Camilla choosing to send a single guard this time, and gives Bill and Shimano instructions for the classes. 

In the early hours of the twelfth day, Halkenburg's body dies. "Balsamilco" reports the matter to Benjamin and lets Benjamin know when the body would be transferred from Tier 3 to the morgue on Tier 1, and the path it would take. He also tells Benjamin of Halkenburg three last wishes—a ship-wide announcement, a send-off by his supporters, and for his personal guards to be his pallbearers. Benjamin instructs Balsamilco to do as he wished, and "Balsamilco" lets Benjamin know that he would return at the same time as Halkenburg's body did. 

On Tier 3, the announcement is made of Halkenburg's death. The public is notified that his funeral procession would take place at noon through Tier 3's central staircase. Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan hear the announcement, and confirm that he was not a benefactor for either mafia before deciding to continue. Phinks and Feitan want to return to Tier 4, but Nobunaga points out that they had some time because of how Hinrigh had hid his transmitter, and says that there's something he wants to check with the other two families before going to the Heil-Ly hideout. 

In a casino on Tier 1, Hisoka half-heartedly plays and wins some games as he thinks about his feelings of arousal around battle. Coincidentally, Bonolenov comes across Hisoka, while disguised as Hisoka himself. He keeps a distance away and remains unnoticed, and recalls the instructions Chrollo gave him, to disguise himself as Hisoka so that the mafia would mistakenly think that they had found Hisoka, and allow the Phantom Troupe to freely search for Hisoka under the incorrect assumption that they had prevented the Troupe from finding him. Bonolenov quickly heads to a bathroom to transform into Owl, the Shadow Beast, and continues strolling around the casino. As he walks around, he thinks about his battle with a woman whose punch could reveal his inner voice who he had to kill, and an I.V. drip guy, who he let live so that the mafia could find him and "convince" him to move upstairs. He worries that the mafia might come after him and tries to figure out if they would or not, but cannot come to a conclusion and gives up in mild frustration. His thoughts drift to Chrollo and Chrollo's unresolved feelings about Shalnark and Kortopi's deaths, and hopes that Hisoka will be the last for the Troupe, and that they could close the curtain on their performance after. Chrollo himself is seen walking around a lower tier as a news item is played remembering the philanthropist Risnorth, hearing the crowd around him scorn the news item being played. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 

Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan turn up at the Cha-R family hideout on Tier 5 and speak with Ken'i and Tajao. Nobunaga asks how much they knew about the current Heil-Ly, and shares his suspicion that the Heil-Ly on board the ship were not the family that the mafia had a shared history with. The spiders learn that a young woman named Morena had become the new boss of the Heil-Ly through an official handover ceremony attended by the higher-ups of the Cha-R, but admit that they are not so close with Morena that they know what she wants. Nobunaga shares his experience fighting with the Heil-Ly, and convinces Tajao that the Heil-Ly on board the ship were killing indiscriminately in order to simultaneously destroy everything, and get the power to destroy everything. Tajao officially asks the spiders to crush the Heil-Ly, letting them know that the Cha-R and Xi-Yu would fully support them, and opens the secret door in the Cha-R base to give them a route to the Heil-Ly. Ken'i watches them enter the door, and begins to worry that the spiders might find Morena sooner than expected. 

In the Heil-Ly secret hideout, Morena lets her underlings know that Halkenburg's funeral procession is taking place. Dogman tells her that he is ready, and explains that after leveling up, he gained the ability to identify people and their Nen proficiencies depending on how close he gets to them. Bille and Gelato cheer in excitement, and Sodom and Dogman make quick plans on how Sodom will abduct the target Dogman identifies as they head out. Voconte asks where the others went, and Morena lets him know that Quorolle, Daemon, and Tevelares were in processing leveling up to get revenge on Hinrigh. 

Tajao leads the spiders down a dark and narrow tunnel. As they walk, he lets Nobunaga know that he would keep Nobunaga's sword for him when they return. Nobunaga kicks up a small fuss at being called out, but continues down the hallway. Tajao opens the door and they step into a big chamber, with a shoddy staircase without railings or end in sight leading upwards. He explains that the stairs are next to the hull of the black whale, and that the stairs would lead them to the top of Tier 2. Phinks comments on the smell in the chamber, and Tajao explains that the chamber is dripping with the waste of the 200,000 people on board, treated at the recycling and sewage treatment facilities between Tiers 4 and 5. He confirms for Nobunaga that the Cha-R and Xi-Yu manage the treatment plants, and Nobunaga suggests that the Heil-Ly were posing as subcontractors, to find prey to kill. From Tajao's shocked reaction, Nobunaga guesses that he is correct and turns around to find them, happy to be right. Before he leaves, he tells Phinks and Feitan some tips about the Heil-Ly he encountered. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 

Hinrigh regroups with Zakuro and walks through Tier 3. They comment that the areas around them are getting crowded with people who want to send off Halkenburg. As they talk about getting Lynch to find out more about the Heil-Ly, a Xi-Yu underling runs up to them and lets them know that Lynch's corpse had been found. Hinrigh and Zakuro go to retrieve her corpse from the military, and Hinrigh gives them a bribe as thanks for contacting them and not writing the body up. They discuss who it could have been as they walk back, and though conscious of the fact that their theories lack evidence, come to the conclusion that someone likely had disguised themselves as Hisoka to trick the mafia into bringing him up, so that the mafia would let their side search for Hisoka in the belief that their search would be in vain. Hinrigh proposes an angry but calm Zakuro that they would avenge her death, even if the Phantom Troupe or Cha-R were behind it. 

Chrollo walks through the crowds gathering for Halkenburg's funeral procession while unsuccessfully using Love Dial 6700 to try and locate his "ideal partner". He thinks about going to Tier 1, guessing that the succession war held there should mean more people with abilities hiding. His thoughts wander to Kakin's three sacred treasures used in the succession battle which he seeks. He assumes that the royal family are already aware of the Phantom Troupe's presence on board, and from the fact that their presence did not elicit much reaction, guesses that the treasures are on upper tiers enshrined in a focal point, a funeral hall, owing to their status as the root of Kakin's prosperity. Chrollo slowly becomes incensed. He seeks to upgrade Skill Hunter by stealing the treasures, giving him the power he needs to kill Hisoka for good. Chrollo reminds himself to not be impatient, and considers the risk of running into Hisoka before being ready to fight while attempting to steal the treasures. He unsuccessfully uses Love Dial 6700 again among the crowd gathered at the general hospital as Halkenburg's coffin is carried out, and concludes what he seeks is on the higher tiers.

Story Impact

The Kakin Empire declares its intent to explore the Dark Continent.
The Black Whale 1 is introduced, the vessel upon which the titular Succession Contest happens, between the 14 Princes of the Kakin Empire to determine who the next King will be. 
Beyond Netero, the leader of Kakin's expedition, is introduced as the son of Isaac Netero. 
Ging and Pariston both leave the Zodiacs, and are replaced by Leorio Paradinight and Kurapika respectively 
Both Pariston and Ging join Beyond's Dark Continent Expedition Team. 
Kurapika and Mizaistom discover that Saiyu is working as an informant for Beyond and Pariston, though Beyond is unaware of his existence. 
The V5 is introduced and commission the Hunter Association to hunt Beyond 
The Five Threats are introduced as punishment for humanity's past attempts at exploring the Dark Continent, through a book titled Journey to the New World and authored by Don Freecss, resulting in the V5's agreement to not travel to the Dark Continent 200 years ago. 
The Kakin Empire is invited to join the V5 and accepts. The V5 reorganize into the V6 to include Kakin and allow them to continue on their journey to the Dark Continent on condition that they find a safe, uninhabited island to act as the Dark Continent for its civilians. 
The V6 change their commission to the Hunter Association from hunting Beyond to becoming his chaperone on the Dark Continent. 
Isaac Netero, through a video recording, requests (orders) the Hunter Association to successfully explore the Dark Continent before Beyond. 
The Black Whale is introduced as the vessel for Kakin's voyage. 
It’s revealed that Kurapika has recovered every pair of Scarlet Eyes except those owned by 4 th Prince Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou. 
Gon learns that he can no longer use Nen and returns to Whale Island. 
The 289 th Hunter Exam is conducted. Only those necessary for the Hunter Association's journey to the Dark Continent are intended to pass. The secondary aim of the exam is to prevent Beyond's associates from infiltrating the Hunter Association and becoming manpower for him on the Dark Continent. 
King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou of the Kakin Empire declares that the generational Succession Contest will take place on the Black Whale 1 during its voyage to the Dark Continent, with the sole survivor being the next king of the Kakin Empire. All 14 of Kakin's Princes participate. 
The Seed Urn Ceremony and Guardian Spirit Beasts are introduced. 
The Hunter Association restricted each Prince to 15 employees. 
Princes may have their guards be Provisional Hunters (Hunters who passed the 289 th Hunter Exam 
After being defeated and killed by Chrollo at Heavens Arena, Hisoka uses post-mortem Nen to revive himself. After his revival, Hisoka makes his goal to kill all of the Spiders, and subsequently kills both Kortopi and Shalnark. 
The Phantom Troupe board the Black Whale to steal its treasure. On board the Black Whale, they change their goals to prioritize killing Hisoka. 
The numbers and full names of every current Phantom Troupe member are revealed. 
Illumi Zoldyck is revealed to have joined the Phantom Troupe. Hisoka had commissioned Illumi to join the Phantom Troupe and to kill Hisoka himself. 
More is revealed about Kurapika's abilities.
Kurapika's Steal Chain and Stealth Dolphin abilities are introduced. Steal Chain was added sometime after the events of Yorknew City. 
It’s revealed that every second Kurapika spends in Emperor Time shortens his life by one hour. This means that twelve hours spent in Emperor Time costs Kurapika five years of his lifespan. 
Kakin's three big mafia families are introduced

Trivia

It is currently the second-longest story arc in the series though it is still being written, behind the Chimera Ant Arc which has 133 chapters.
The concept of "Nen after death", "post-mortem Nen", or "Nen becoming stronger after death" is very prevalent in the Succession Contest arc, as in:
The Seed Urn, a conjured object that outlives its creator. 
The Sun and Moon, a Nen ability that continued to exist in Chrollo's book even after the original owner died. 
Hisoka's use of his Bungee Gum to reanimate his body after he died. 
Benjamin's Nen ability, Benjamin Baton, that allows him to inherit the Nen abilities of his soldiers when they die. 
Camilla's counteractive Nen ability, Cat's Name, that activates after she is killed. 
The Nen ability of Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast, Without You, whose activation is triggered by the death of Kacho or Fugetsu and starts to work from then on. 
The Have-Nots' Nen ability, Yomotsu Hegui, a Nen curse triggered by, among other things, the user's suicide. 
Misha Hao's post-Mortem Nen ability Zombie Girl, activated upon her death, which enables her to manipulate corpses and dispose of them. 
The curse that Beyond's children carry as a result of his Nen attack on them during their infancy, believed to be activated upon their deaths. These curses vary in strength and their targets are unknown. 

Intertextuality and References

This arc is characterized by a plethora of references to songs or performances by the Japanese idol group Keyakizaka46:
The Silent Majority Nen ability has the same name as Keyakizaka46's debut single. The doll's hairstyle may also be considered reminiscent of the center girl of the song.
The name of Camilla's ability, "Neko no Namae", is also the title of a song by the idol group.
The sound made by Camilla's Nen Beast, "neru", is the onomatopoeia for kneading, but it is also the name of one of the group's members, Nagahama Neru.
In Chapter 378, the item numbers "Silent Majority" (SRCL 9041) and "Masshiro na Mono wa Yogoshitaku naru" (SRCL 9488) can be seen on a sign in Tier 3.
Halkenburg's pose when notching his arrow in Chapter 382 is taken from the music video of "Fukyouwaon".
The servants dancing in Chapter 383 are seen striking a pose similar to one from the concert "Keyaki Kyowakoku 2017", a similarity which is reinforced by the fact that the flag in the background is identical to the ones waved by the group.
The second name of Kacho’s Guardian Spirit Beast's ability, "Season of Two" (Futari Saison), is a reference to their third single, and the main name, "Without You", comes from the fourth track (Kimi ga Inai) from the "Masshirona Mono wa Yogoshitaku Naru" album of the same idol group.
Bill's Nen ability, Erigeron, is named after a song by Nogizaka46, Keyakizaka46's associated group.
A few scenes in chapter 236 of the Me & Roboco manga series parody the Chrollo vs Hisoka fight in Heaven's Arena.
